description: Tomato leaf mold is a fungal disease particularly important in greenhouses and protected cultivation where humidity remains high.
symptoms: Pale green or yellow spots develop on upper leaf surfaces
Olive-green to brown velvety fungal growth develops on leaf undersides
Affected leaves may curl, dry, and die
Severe infection can reduce photosynthesis and plant vigor

favorable_conditions.additional_conditions: Poor ventilation and dense plant canopies favor disease

spread: Movement of spores within greenhouse environments

biological_control: Use locally registered biological fungicides where appropriate

chemical_control.recommended: Registered fungicides may be used when environmental management alone does not provide adequate control.
chemical_control.warning: Verify local registration and greenhouse-use restrictions before application.

severity_guidance.low: Localized symptoms. Reduce humidity and monitor.
severity_guidance.moderate: Multiple leaves affected. Remove infected tissue and improve ventilation.
severity_guidance.high: Widespread infection. Combine environmental control with locally recommended disease management.

farmer_advice: Humidity management and ventilation are among the most important controls for tomato leaf mold, especially in greenhouse cultivation.
disclaimer: Confirm diagnosis when symptoms are unclear because several tomato diseases can cause yellow leaf spots.
